article_meta.title: Defense Coast Guard denies report it won't classify swastikas, nooses as hate symbols by Filip Timotija 11/20/25 03:23 P

reasoning: The model returned an empty array. The article is largely composed of quoted statements and attributed source language, which makes it lean toward being neutrally reported. However, there are some arguable bias instances in the author's own prose that could have been flagged. The most notable is the author's descriptive parenthetical 'widely recognized as a symbol of the German Nazi Party and linked to the killing of millions of Jews' which is author-added context/editorializing that goes beyond neutral reporting of the Post's claims. This could be seen as Sensationalism/Emotionalism or Spin - adding emotional weight to what is a factual description but in context serves to frame the Coast Guard's policy change more negatively. However, one could also argue this is factual context. The article also structurally gives significant space to the Washington Post's reporting and less emphasis to the Coast Guard's denial despite the headline framing it as the Coast Guard denying the report - this could be seen as Slant. The empty array is defensible but misses at least 1-2 arguable instances.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The model returned an empty array, so there are zero attribution violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: No items were flagged, so there are no false positives.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed some arguable bias instances: (1) The author's own descriptive clause 'widely recognized as a symbol of the German Nazi Party and linked to the killing of millions of Jews' is editorial context added by the author that could be flagged as Sensationalism/Emotionalism or editorializing. (2) There's an argument for Slant in how the article structures the Coast Guard denial vs. the Post's reporting details. However, this article is primarily composed of quoted/attributed material and these instances are debatable, so the miss is moderate rather than severe.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: No bias types were applied, so no accuracy issues.
